ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pork head salad or Burmese-style pork head salad. FOODON_03302757 (pork food product) is used as the closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pig meat and products thereof) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for pork head salad as a prepared dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for pork head salad or Burmese pork head salad as a specific food entity.

preparation_method: dressed with lime juice and fish sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Pork Head Salad is approximately 200 g, served as a starter or side dish on a plate, comprising sliced boiled pork head meat tossed with herbs, aromatics, and dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, head, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for raw pork head meat (FDC ID 168370), adjusted for boiling and the addition of typical salad dressing components (lime juice, fish sauce, shallots, herbs, peanuts). Energy and macros reflect the composite dish estimated at approximately 60% pork head meat by weight with remaining mass from vegetables, herbs, and dressing. Protein and fat values are moderated from raw pork head to account for cooking losses and dilution by low-calorie salad components. This is an estimated composite; no exact match exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific Burmese dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Burmese pork head salad. GI is estimated at 32 based on analogous mixed meat salad with low-carbohydrate dressing. Pork and protein-dominant dishes with minimal starchy carbohydrate consistently show GI in the 25–40 range per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (32 × 4.2 × 2) / 100 = 2.7, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and seasoning (~420 mg sodium per 100 g; ~840 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced fish sauce, as a 200 g serving contributes approximately 840 mg sodium, representing 37% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~32) and low carbohydrate content (~4.2 g per 100 g) with high protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pork Head Salad has a low glycemic index of approximately 32 and a glycemic load of 2.7 per serving, making it a suitable protein-rich option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugary dressings.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.8 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~72 mg per 100 g) from pork head meat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the saturated fat and cholesterol content from pork head meat; limiting serving frequency and pairing with additional vegetables can help manage overall saturated fat intake within the AHA-recommended limit of less than 13 g per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork Head Salad (ဝက်ခေါင်းသုပ်, Wek Kaung Thoke) is a traditional Burmese cold salad made from boiled and thinly sliced pork head meat — including cuts from the cheek, jowl, snout, and ear — tossed with a vibrant dressing of lime juice, fish sauce, sliced shallots, minced garlic, lemongrass, fresh chilli, coriander, mint, roasted peanuts, and toasted sesame seeds. The dish is a staple of Burmese cuisine and is commonly served as a starter, side dish, or street food snack. The texture is a combination of tender boiled pork meat and the slight chewiness of ear cartilage, balanced by the crunch of peanuts and the brightness of fresh herbs. The flavour profile is sour, savoury, and mildly spicy, characteristic of Burmese-style thoke (salad) dishes. Nutritionally, Pork Head Salad is a moderate-calorie, protein-rich dish (~148 kcal and ~14 g protein per 100 g) with relatively low carbohydrate content (~4.2 g per 100 g), giving it a low glycemic index of approximately 32. The sodium content is notable (~420 mg per 100 g) due to fish sauce, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The dish contains moderate saturated fat and cholesterol from the pork head meat, warranting attention for those with cardiovascular disease. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. In Singapore, this dish appears on the menus of Burmese and Southeast Asian cafes and is sometimes listed under Myanmar cuisine offerings.
common_user_queries: How many calories in pork head salad?
Is pork head salad good for diabetics?
What is ဝက်ခေါင်းသုပ်?

What does pork head salad taste like?

confidence_reason: No exact match for Burmese Pork Head Salad exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or ICMR-NIN databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pork head meat data with composite estimation for the salad format. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the primary ingredient (pork head meat) has solid USDA SR Legacy data and the adaptation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Cuisine correction: The input JSON listed cuisine as 'Italian' and food_group as 'Vegetables', which are clearly erroneous for this Burmese pork dish. Cuisine has been corrected to 'Burmese (Myanmar)' and food_group to 'Meat and alternatives' based on the dish identity. 2. The restaurant 'The Light Cafe - High Street Plaza' in Singapore serves this dish, suggesting it is offered in a multi-cuisine cafe context. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for pork head meat and is not reliably estimated for this composite dish. 4. Nutrient values are estimated composites; a direct laboratory analysis of this specific dish as served in Singapore would improve confidence to high. 5. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Burmese pork head salad was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of the accessed date. 6. Sodium content is notably high due to fish sauce; clinical guidance for hypertension and cardiovascular disease reflects this. 7. The dish is not halal due to pork content.
